not_applicable_exceptions.reason: This change removes version-specific paths and preserves the existing paired runner; it makes no Agent-behavior claim.
This workflow adds fail-closed diff classification and durable evidence assets; it does not change an evaluated Agent candidate.
Candidate identifiers now follow the package version; candidate bytes and observed behavior are otherwise unchanged.
This verifier enforces release evidence classification and does not alter an Agent runner, prompt, or candidate.
The manifest records the actual release directory instead of a v1.9 constant; all bound bytes remain verified.
This tracked contract records the reviewed classification, changed-path digest, and non-recursive content Diff digest; its verifier tests reject stale or incomplete declarations.

The default-off activation policy is a declarative authorization contract covered by exact metadata and text tests; the existing paired suites evaluate a different synthetic Trace-routing candidate and would not test non-activation.
The interface metadata mirrors the declarative default-off authorization contract and contains no observed behavior claim.
This fail-closed integrity check rejects unsealed or stale Trace evidence and is covered by deterministic scoring tests.
